goals.g1: Do detect_overlaps/resolve_compound hold up at 3 matches, closing the cascade gap flagged after the 2-match tests?

c1.statement: test 3 (fan-in) produced boundary_crossings={(0,1):{r2},(2,1):{r3}}, redirects={(0,outA):1,(2,outC):1} -- both crossers correctly redirected to owner B independently

c2.statement: test 4 (combined collision+independent crossing) produced overlapping_nodes={n1:{0,1}}, boundary_crossings={(2,1):{n2}}, forced_born={1:{b1}}, redirects={(2,outC):1} -- no interference between the two overlap kinds sharing match B

c3.statement: resolved_owner is populated only for overlapping_nodes because that is the only case with genuine ambiguity; boundary_crossings owner is unambiguous by construction of the (i,j) key, so the .get(n,j) fallback is always correct there

c4.statement: across all 4 tests run, every combination of the two overlap kinds produced correct non-interfering results

c5.statement: true transitive cascade (a crosser whose owner is itself a crosser into a further match) was not constructed in any test -- all tests have at most one hop

c6.statement: DECISION: detect_overlaps/resolve_compound are validated at 3-match fan-in and combined-collision depth. The remaining open gap is specifically multi-hop transitive chains, unverified but structurally expected to work since redirects/forced_born are deferred owner-index pointers resolved at apply time, not precomputed real-node values -- this expectation itself is NOT yet tested and should be revisited once the orchestrator exists (since a real multi-hop chain requires the orchestrator's resolution ORDER, not just detect/resolve's output shape).

derived_from.rule: 4 independent test combinations all passed; remaining gap is scoped precisely to what wasn't constructed, with the reason the deferred-pointer design plausibly generalizes stated as an expectation, not a verified claim

ans.statement: detect_overlaps/resolve_compound validated at 3-match depth (fan-in + combined collision); multi-hop transitive chains remain the one unverified gap, deferred to orchestrator-level testing since it requires resolution ORDER not just detect/resolve output shape.

k1.method: ran both 3-match scenarios, compared to hand-predicted values

k2.method: checked whether any test had an owner that was itself a crosser into a third match

committed.statement: DECISION: detect_overlaps/resolve_compound are validated at 3-match fan-in and combined-collision depth. The remaining open gap is specifically multi-hop transitive chains, unverified but structurally expected to work since redirects/forced_born are deferred owner-index pointers resolved at apply time, not precomputed real-node values -- this expectation itself is NOT yet tested and should be revisited once the orchestrator exists (since a real multi-hop chain requires the orchestrator's resolution ORDER, not just detect/resolve's output shape).
